---
title: "Interactive Degree Planner"
subtitle: "Drag-and-drop course planning with live designation gap analysis"
date: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Status — MVP built, awaiting hosting
The Shiny degree-planner MVP **is functional** in
[`app/app.R` in the project source repository](https://github.com/DrNelsonJatel/UBCO_Freshwater_Science_Program/tree/main/app).
It reads the live UBCO course catalogue and the PAg + RPBio designation
mappings curated on the rest of this site, and reports:

- credits + degree-threshold check (120 / 78 Science / 42 upper /
  36 upper Science)
- PAg category coverage (8 foundational + 20 agrology with 8 senior)
- RPBio category coverage (6 core areas + 5 three-of-five areas) +
  the 13-biology count

Pending: a public hosted URL (shinyapps.io or Cloud Run). Once
deployed, the navbar **Planner** link will point at the live app.
Until then, students can preview the app locally via
`shiny::runApp("app")` from the cloned repository, or email the
program advisor for a guided demo.
:::

# What it will do

When live, the planner will let you:

- **Drag courses** into year slots (Year 1, 2, 3, 4 and post-Year-4
  summer).
- **Toggle designation goals** (PAg, RPBio, neither, both).
- See **live gap analysis**:
  - Which prerequisites are missing for the courses you have chosen.
  - Which BCIA / CAB category requirements are still unfilled.
  - Whether your total credits clear the 120 / 78 Science / 42
    upper-level / 36 upper-level-Science thresholds.
- View the **prerequisite graph** for your selected courses (`visNetwork`).
- **Export your plan** as a PDF or text summary you can share with the
  program advisor.

# In the meantime

- Use the [course catalogue](../courses/index.qmd) for the year-by-year
  course list and prerequisite notes.
- Use the [PAg pathway](../designations/pag.qmd) and
  [RPBio pathway](../designations/rpbio.qmd) for the curated 4-year sample
  plans + designation-fit narrative.
- Use the [Complementary pathways](../designations/complementary.qmd)
  page for Chemistry, Biology, and hydrology/water-resources bundles.

# Want to help shape it?

If you are a Freshwater Science student or instructor and want input
into how the planner should behave — what gap signals matter most, what
visual encoding is most useful, how aggressively it should flag
prerequisite conflicts — please drop a note to **Nelson.Jatel@UBC.ca**
or open a GitHub issue at the
[project source repository](https://github.com/DrNelsonJatel/UBCO_Freshwater_Science_Program/issues).
